what_was_learned: HUP data follows BIDS-style organization with subject folders, presurgery session folders, and iEEG files.
The EDF file contains the actual intracranial EEG time-series signals.
The channel file contains electrode names, channel status, bad channels, sampling frequency, and clinical labels such as SOZ or resect.
The event file tells where seizure onset and offset occur in seconds and samples.
SOZ means seizure onset zone: channels clinically identified as the place where seizures begin.
Bad channels were removed before analysis because they may contain noise or non-useful signals.
Raw iEEG signals were converted into numerical features such as RMS, peak-to-peak amplitude, line length, and band power.
Delta, theta, alpha, beta, gamma, and high-gamma power summarize how much signal energy exists in different frequency ranges.
All three ictal runs were aligned around seizure onset to compare preictal, ictal, and postictal behavior fairly.
Gamma and high-gamma power increased in SOZ channels after seizure onset, especially in run-01 and run-02.
